name: tickets
description: IT support tickets. One row per case. Conversation turns live in the conversations datastore, linked via ticketId (the ticketId is also the subfolder name under databases/conversations/).

fields.label: Subject
fields.comment: Short summary of the question — used in lists and banners.

fields.label: Description
fields.comment: Full text of what the user asked. Subsequent back-and-forth lives in openit-conversations, not here.

fields.label: From
fields.comment: Name or email. 'unknown' if the asker isn't identifiable.

fields.label: Channel
fields.comment: How the question reached us. 'chat' = the localhost chat-intake form. Other channels available when cloud is connected (channel ingest).

fields.label: Status
fields.comment: Lifecycle states: agent-responding = claude -p subprocess actively running this turn — admin sees the activity banner ONLY in this state. Set on first ticket creation (skipping a separate 'incoming' state) and again on every follow-up turn before the subprocess starts. open = agent finished its turn (KB hit, answered the asker), conversation alive but the agent is idle waiting for the asker's next message; no banner. escalated = admin needs to look at this. Triggered by any of four paths: (1) agent emits <<STATUS:escalated>> (KB miss / agent gave up), (2) claude -p subprocess crashed mid-turn, (3) admin replies on the ticket — the agent is no longer the sole driver, (4) ticket sat in 'open' past the auto-escalate threshold (default 24h, configurable via .openit/config.json). escalated banner fires for the admin. resolved = the asker's question has been fully answered and the conversation is done (set by admin via Mark-as-resolved, or by the agent emitting <<STATUS:resolved>>). resolved is reopenable — a follow-up asker turn flips back to agent-responding and the agent re-decides. closed = no further action; archived. Set by the auto-close walker after a ticket has been resolved past the threshold (default 24h, configurable via .openit/config.json).

fields.label: Assigned to
fields.comment: Name or email of the admin handling this ticket. Null if unassigned.

fields.label: KB articles cited
fields.comment: Repo-relative paths (e.g. `knowledge-bases/default/foo.md`) used to answer this ticket. Helps the 'answer once' loop stay traceable across multiple KB collections.

fields.label: Internal notes
fields.comment: Admin-only notes not surfaced in the user-facing reply. E.g. 'declined to capture as KB — one-off issue specific to this user's machine.'

fields.label: Slack workspace
fields.comment: Slack team_id (e.g. 'T012ABCDE'). Set on first turn for tickets with askerChannel: 'slack'. Used by the listener's egress polling loop to confirm the ticket originated from the same workspace it's currently connected to.

fields.label: Slack DM channel
fields.comment: Slack DM channel id (starts with 'D'). Set on first turn for tickets with askerChannel: 'slack'. The local Slack listener uses this for outbound delivery — admin replies on this ticket get DMed to the asker via this channel.

fields.label: Slack user
fields.comment: Slack user id of the asker (starts with 'U'). Set on first turn for tickets with askerChannel: 'slack'. Used by the listener for the defensive 30-min ticket-resume lookup after a restart.
